description: Professional and consumer hair-care brand specializing in bond-repair technology. Sold through salons and prestige retail. IPO'd Sep 2021 at $21; stock collapsed amid sales declines and 2023 class-action over hair-loss claims.
context: Bloomberg leaked Henkel talks 1/7/2026 (stock jumped 22-36%). Advent International (~75%) selling. Strategic — Henkel adds to consumer-beauty portfolio.

description: U.S. retail pharmacy chain (~8,500 Walgreens stores) and international pharmacy/health/beauty businesses (Boots UK, etc.). Long-struggling under pressure from PBMs, declining front-of-store, and 2024 Village Medical write-down.
context: Bloomberg reported takeover talks 12/10/2024, sending stock up; rumors persisted through Q1 2025. Pessina family (~17%) rolled their entire stake. Cash $11.45 + up to $3.00 DAP Right tied to VillageMD/Shields/CareCentrix divestitures.

acquirer: Restaurant Brands International (RBI) — Burger King parent
description: Largest Burger King franchisee in the U.S. — operated ~1,000 BK restaurants plus ~60 Popeyes locations. Public-company franchisee was unusual structure; RBI already held ~15% stake.
context: Strategic re-acquisition by franchisor of largest franchisee. RBI cited refranchising strategy and operational improvements. 23.1% premium to 30-day VWAP, 13.4% to 1/12/2024 close. Friendly board process (no auction).

outcomeReason: Macy's board TERMINATED talks 7/15/2024: inadequate financing certainty (real-estate-tied) + value below board threshold
description: Largest U.S. department store chain — Macy's, Bloomingdale's, Bluemercury. ~700 stores. Struggling with secular mall decline; mid-turnaround under CEO Tony Spring's 'A Bold New Chapter' strategy (150 store closures planned).
context: Arkhouse/Brigade initially bid $21 (Dec 2023, rejected). Raised to $24 (March 2024), then $24.80 (June 2024). Macy's board terminated 7/15/2024 citing inadequate financing certainty (asset-based real estate financing tied to store appraisals) and insufficient value. Stock dropped ~14% on termination.

description: Online marketplace for pet care services — dog/cat boarding, walking, sitting, daycare. Founded 2011, public via 2021 SPAC merger with Nebula Caravel.
context: Friendly process; no leak prior to announcement. 29.4% premium to 11/28/2023 close, 61% premium to 90-day VWAP. 30-day go-shop period yielded no superior bids. CEO Easterly remained.

description: Women's apparel retailer operating Chico's, White House Black Market, and Soma brands. ~1,260 boutiques in U.S. Long-struggling mall-based specialty retail.
context: Sycamore Partners (also acquirer of WBA, Loft, Talbots) added Chico's to retail portfolio. 65% premium to 9/27/2023 unaffected close — very large premium reflecting stock's prolonged weakness. Friendly.

description: Sweet baked goods producer — Twinkies, Ding Dongs, Ho Hos, Donettes. Re-public via 2016 SPAC merger after famous 2012 bankruptcy and Apollo/Metropoulos restructuring.
context: Reuters reported takeover speculation 8/25/2023 (multiple bidders rumored). Smucker won at $34.25 ($30 cash + 0.03002 SJM shares). 54% premium to 8/24/2023 unaffected. Friendly auction.

outcomeReason: BLOCKED by FTC/SDNY antitrust ruling 10/24/2024; agreement terminated 11/14/2024
description: Luxury fashion conglomerate; brands include Michael Kors (largest), Versace, and Jimmy Choo. Competes with LVMH, Kering, and target acquirer Tapestry (Coach, Kate Spade).
context: Tapestry announced $8.5B all-cash acquisition 8/10/2023 at $57/share (~59% premium to unaffected $35.78). FTC sued to block 4/22/2024 citing 'accessible luxury handbag' market concentration. SDNY Judge Rochon granted preliminary injunction 10/24/2024. Stock cratered ~50% on ruling.

description: Premium/specialty food brands holding company — flagship Rao's Homemade pasta sauce (most of value), Michael Angelo's frozen Italian, Noosa yoghurt (later divested), Birch Benders pancake mix. Advent International created the platform 2017 via roll-up.
context: Advent (~58% pre-deal holder) ran sale process. Campbell paid 28% premium to 8/4/2023 unaffected close. Friendly. Stock had recovered from 2022 lows on Rao's continued strength.

acquirer: Solenis (Platinum Equity portfolio company) — strategic merger
description: Hygiene, infection prevention, and cleaning solutions for institutional customers (hospitality, healthcare, food service, retail). Bain Capital was controlling shareholder (~73%) from 2017 buyout from Sealed Air.
context: Strategic combination with Solenis (water treatment / process chemicals, Platinum Equity portfolio). Public holders received $8.40; Bain rolled at $7.84. 41% premium to 3/7/2023 unaffected, 59% to 90-day VWAP. Bain retained equity in combined entity.
